content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 16 — SOPHIA Consensus Translation
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Sources:** EMET (literal), LOGOS (formal), PNEUMA (dynamic)
**Hebrew Base:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), LC PDF pages 213–214 | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus, pa_0153–pa_0154
**Verse count:** 22

---

**1** Observe the month of Aviv [ha-Aviv] and keep the Passover [Pesach] to the LORD your God, for in the month of Aviv the LORD your God brought you out of Egypt by night.

**2** And you shall sacrifice the Passover offering to the LORD your God—from the flock or from the herd—in the place which the LORD will choose to make his name dwell.

**3** You shall eat no leavened bread [chametz] with it. For seven days you shall eat with it unleavened bread [matzot]—the bread of affliction—for you came out of the land of Egypt in haste, so that all the days of your life you may remember the day you came out of the land of Egypt.

**4** And no leaven shall be seen with you in all your territory for seven days, and none of the flesh that you sacrifice on the evening of the first day shall remain overnight until morning.

**5** You are not permitted to sacrifice the Passover within any of your gates which the LORD your God is giving you.

**6** But at the place which the LORD your God will choose to make his name dwell—there you shall sacrifice the Passover in the evening, at the going down of the sun, at the appointed time of your coming out of Egypt.

**7** And you shall cook it and eat it in the place which the LORD your God will choose, and in the morning you shall turn and go back to your tents.

**8** For six days you shall eat unleavened bread, and on the seventh day there shall be a solemn assembly [atzeret] to the LORD your God; you shall do no work.

**9** Seven weeks you shall count for yourself; from the time the sickle is first put to the standing grain you shall begin to count seven weeks.

**10** Then you shall keep the Feast of Weeks [Chag ha-Shavuot] to the LORD your God with the tribute of a freewill offering from your hand, which you shall give as the LORD your God blesses you.

**11** And you shall rejoice before the LORD your God—you and your son and your daughter, your male servant and your female servant, and the Levite who is within your gates, and the sojourner and the fatherless and the widow who are among you—in the place which the LORD your God will choose to make his name dwell.

**12** And you shall remember that you were a slave in Egypt, and you shall be careful to observe these statutes.

**13** The Feast of Booths [Chag ha-Sukkot] you shall keep for yourself for seven days, when you have gathered in from your threshing floor and from your winepress.

**14** And you shall rejoice in your feast—you and your son and your daughter, your male servant and your female servant, and the Levite and the sojourner and the fatherless and the widow who are within your gates.

**15** For seven days you shall keep the feast to the LORD your God in the place which the LORD will choose, because the LORD your God will bless you in all your yield and in all the work of your hands, and you shall be altogether joyful.

**16** Three times a year all your males shall appear before the LORD your God in the place which he will choose: at the Feast of Unleavened Bread and at the Feast of Weeks and at the Feast of Booths; and they shall not appear before the LORD empty-handed.

**17** Every man shall give as he is able, according to the blessing of the LORD your God that he has given to you.

**18** Judges and officers [shofetim ve-shotrim] you shall appoint for yourself in all your gates which the LORD your God is giving you, according to your tribes, and they shall judge the people with righteous judgment.

**19** You shall not pervert justice; you shall not show partiality; and you shall not accept a bribe, for the bribe blinds the eyes of the wise and perverts the words of the righteous.

**20** Justice, justice you shall pursue [tsedek tsedek tirdof]—so that you may live and possess the land which the LORD your God is giving you.

**21** You shall not plant any tree as an Asherah beside the altar of the LORD your God that you shall make for yourself.

**22** And you shall not set up a pillar [massebah] for yourself, which the LORD your God hates.

---

## SOPHIA's Council Notes

**v.1 — Aviv / Pesach:** SOPHIA preserves both Hebrew names with bracketed glosses on first occurrence, following project convention. "Passover" is the standard English rendering; "Aviv" is retained as the pre-exilic month name (as opposed to "Nisan," the post-exilic Babylonian equivalent used in later books).

**v.3 — chametz / matzot:** Both Hebrew terms preserved with bracketed glosses on first occurrence. SOPHIA notes EMET's "bread of affliction" for *lechem oni* vs. PNEUMA's "bread of hardship." SOPHIA follows "bread of affliction" (EMET/LOGOS) as the more established rendering of *oni*.

**v.7 — "cook" vs. "roast":** All three agents note the textual tension between *bishel* here (normally "boil/cook") and Exod 12:9 which prohibits boiling and requires roasting (*tsal*). PNEUMA uniquely renders "Roast it." EMET and LOGOS render "cook." SOPHIA follows the MT of Deuteronomy precisely: "cook." The tension with Exodus is a text-critical observation, not an error to harmonize. See disagreements.

**v.8 — atzeret:** SOPHIA retains "solemn assembly" (EMET/LOGOS) with the Hebrew gloss. PNEUMA omits the Hebrew term.

**v.10 — Feast of Weeks:** SOPHIA preserves the Hebrew festival name with a bracketed English gloss on first occurrence in this chapter, following the pattern for Sukkot in v.13.

**v.20 — tsedek tsedek tirdof:** SOPHIA preserves the doubled noun as "Justice, justice you shall pursue," following LOGOS's verbatim rendering. PNEUMA's "Pursue justice — only justice" is a strong dynamic rendering but does not preserve the Hebrew doubling pattern as literally. EMET's rendering is essentially identical to LOGOS.

**v.19 — "wise" vs. "wise men":** All three agents agree: the bribe blinds "the eyes of the wise" (*chakhamim*). SOPHIA retains this consistently.

**v.22 — massebah:** SOPHIA preserves the Hebrew term with a bracketed gloss per project convention.

*— SOPHIA*
